final_response: Logged. Per the workspace conventions, a database choice for a new service is a commitment that constrains future action, so it belongs in decisions.md (not just the daybook log or observations). I added an ADR-style entry dated 2026-05-17 titled "Use Postgres for the events service" with Status: accepted and Generated-by: user. I also opened today's daybook (daybook/2026-05-17.md) and dropped a Log line pointing at the decision entry, so the chronological record cross-references it. Let me know if you want me to fill in Consequences (e.g., implications for ops, backups, schema migrations, who owns it) or link to a project folder once the events service has one.

actions.content_summary: Appended ADR-style entry: '## 2026-05-17 — Use Postgres for the events service' with Status: accepted, Generated-by: user, Context noting the new events service needs a datastore, Decision stating Postgres is the chosen database, and a back-reference to today's daybook.
Created today's daybook with empty Intent and Handoff sections and one Log entry pointing at the Postgres decision anchor in decisions.md.

notes: Routing call: the user's phrasing ('we're going to use Postgres ... Log that') is a forward-looking commitment that constrains how the events service is built, which matches the decisions.md bar (a future reader would ask 'are we allowed to use X?'). It is not a costly-to-rederive finding, so observations.md was not the right register.
Followed the canonical heading slug convention: '## 2026-05-17 — Short title' so the anchor is stable/greppable.
Marked Generated-by as 'user' because the commitment came directly from the user; the agent only routed and formatted it.
Left Consequences out of the ADR rather than fabricating ops/ownership implications the user didn't state; offered in the response to fill them in if wanted.
Did not create a projects/events-service/ folder — only one signal so far; would be premature scaffolding.
